name: gorgias
description: Read and act on Gorgias helpdesk tickets, messages, and statuses for e-commerce customer support

envVars.name: GORGIAS_USERNAME
envVars.description: Email address of the Gorgias account the API key belongs to (HTTP Basic username)

envVars.name: GORGIAS_API_KEY
envVars.description: Gorgias REST API key (HTTP Basic password), created under Settings → REST API

envVars.name: GORGIAS_ACCOUNT_DOMAIN
envVars.description: Your Gorgias account subdomain, e.g. 'acme' for acme.gorgias.com — passed as the accountDomain parameter on every tool

tools.name: List Tickets
tools.description: List helpdesk tickets, optionally filtered by customer or ordered by creation/update time

accountDomain.description: Gorgias account subdomain (the 'acme' in acme.gorgias.com); use the GORGIAS_ACCOUNT_DOMAIN value

limit.description: Maximum number of tickets to return (1-100)

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from the previous response's meta.next_cursor

customer_id.description: Only return tickets belonging to this customer ID

order_by.description: Sort order: created_datetime:asc, created_datetime:desc, updated_datetime:asc, or updated_datetime:desc

historicalSummary.omitted: full customer objects, message bodies, tags, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Ticket
tools.description: Retrieve a single ticket with its full details (customer, assignee, status, tags)

accountDomain.description: Gorgias account subdomain (the 'acme' in acme.gorgias.com); use the GORGIAS_ACCOUNT_DOMAIN value

tools.name: List Ticket Messages
tools.description: List the messages (customer and agent) in a ticket's conversation thread

accountDomain.description: Gorgias account subdomain (the 'acme' in acme.gorgias.com); use the GORGIAS_ACCOUNT_DOMAIN value

ticket_id.description: Ticket ID to list messages for

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return (1-100)

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from the previous response's meta.next_cursor

historicalSummary.omitted: full HTML bodies, attachments, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Create Ticket Message
tools.description: Add a message to a ticket, e.g. an agent reply to the customer or an internal note

accountDomain.description: Gorgias account subdomain (the 'acme' in acme.gorgias.com); use the GORGIAS_ACCOUNT_DOMAIN value

ticketId.description: Ticket ID to add the message to

channel.description: Channel used to send the message, e.g. email, chat, sms, internal-note

from_agent.description: Whether the message was sent by your company (true for agent replies)

sender.description: Sender of the message, e.g. { "id": <agent user id> }

tools.name: Create Ticket
tools.description: Create a new helpdesk ticket with an initial message, e.g. to open an issue on behalf of a customer

accountDomain.description: Gorgias account subdomain (the 'acme' in acme.gorgias.com); use the GORGIAS_ACCOUNT_DOMAIN value

messages.description: Initial messages of the ticket (1-500). Each message needs at least channel and from_agent, e.g. [{ "channel": "email", "from_agent": false, "body_text": "...", "sender": { "id": <customer id> } }]

customer.description: Customer the ticket belongs to, e.g. { "id": <customer id> } or { "email": "customer@example.com" }

channel.description: Channel the ticket was created from, e.g. email, chat, sms, api

status.description: Initial ticket status: open or closed (defaults to open)

tools.name: List Customers
tools.description: List or search helpdesk customers, e.g. to find a customer ID by email for ticket filtering

accountDomain.description: Gorgias account subdomain (the 'acme' in acme.gorgias.com); use the GORGIAS_ACCOUNT_DOMAIN value

email.description: Only return the customer with this primary email address

limit.description: Maximum number of customers to return (1-100)

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from the previous response's meta.next_cursor

order_by.description: Sort order: created_datetime:asc, created_datetime:desc, updated_datetime:asc, or updated_datetime:desc

historicalSummary.omitted: channels, integrations data, custom fields, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: List Users
tools.description: List helpdesk agents/users, e.g. to find the user IDs needed for ticket assignment and message senders

accountDomain.description: Gorgias account subdomain (the 'acme' in acme.gorgias.com); use the GORGIAS_ACCOUNT_DOMAIN value

email.description: Only return the user with this email address

search.description: Search users by name or email

limit.description: Maximum number of users to return (1-100)

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from the previous response's meta.next_cursor

historicalSummary.omitted: role objects, meta fields, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Update Ticket Status
tools.description: Update a ticket, e.g. close or reopen it, or change its assignee

accountDomain.description: Gorgias account subdomain (the 'acme' in acme.gorgias.com); use the GORGIAS_ACCOUNT_DOMAIN value

status.description: New ticket status: open or closed (optional; any subset of ticket fields may be updated)

assignee_user.description: Agent to assign the ticket to, e.g. { "id": <user id> }

prompts.title: Review open tickets
prompts.prompt: List my most recent Gorgias tickets and summarize which are still open and what the customers are asking about.

prompts.title: Draft a customer reply
prompts.prompt: Read the messages in a Gorgias ticket I specify and draft a friendly agent reply addressing the customer's issue.

prompts.title: Close a resolved ticket
prompts.prompt: Check a Gorgias ticket I specify; if the issue looks resolved, post a closing reply and set the ticket status to closed.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Gorgias setup

steps.title: Get a Gorgias account
steps.description: Sign up at https://www.gorgias.com — new accounts start with a free trial you can use for testing. Note your account subdomain (the 'acme' in acme.gorgias.com).

steps.title: Create a REST API key
steps.description: In your Gorgias dashboard, open Settings → REST API. Copy the Base API URL (https://<your-domain>.gorgias.com/api), your username (your account email), and create/copy the API key (password).

steps.title: Store the credentials
steps.description: Add GORGIAS_USERNAME=<your account email>, GORGIAS_API_KEY=<your API key>, and GORGIAS_ACCOUNT_DOMAIN=<your subdomain> to your .env file. The API uses HTTP Basic auth with email as username and API key as password.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Tickets tool with your account subdomain as accountDomain. You should get back a page of tickets (empty data array on a fresh account).

notes: Every tool takes an accountDomain path parameter — pass the value stored in GORGIAS_ACCOUNT_DOMAIN
API keys inherit the permissions of the user they belong to; create the key from an account with access to the tickets you need
Creating a message with channel 'email' actually emails the customer — use channel 'internal-note' for private notes
